title: Montserrat Roig's Classic Novel The Time of Cherries Finally in English
content:
Montserrat Roig's classic novel, "The Time of Cherries," captures a poignant moment in the history of Barcelona right before profound changes occurred. Originally published in Catalan in 1976, the year after dictator Francisco Franco died, the book explores family life during a period of historical flux. Now, half a century later, this essential piece of post-Franco Catalonia is available in English for the first time. The novel is infused with vivid characters and offers a sharply detailed vision of middle-class Barcelona before democracy was restored. It revolves around Natàlia, a former student activist, and explores the complex relationships between generations.

The middle class in Barcelona had remained largely undisturbed by the major wars of the 20th century. Its members held onto their spacious apartments, maintained their innate conservatism, and quietly looked after their affairs during the dictatorship. However, by the 1960s, things were ripe for a revolt by the younger generation. Roig herself was born into a middle-class family in 1946, but her parents had already staged their own ideological rebellion. Growing up in a progressive household, she was deeply influenced by the cultural and political discussions that took place around her.

In her late teens, Roig began writing about current affairs for a pro-democracy magazine and trained as an actor. During this transformative period, she befriended the prominent feminist writer and left-wing activist Maria Aurèlia Capmany. Capmany introduced the young Roig to radical feminist texts and served as an indomitable, witty mentor. Much like her mentor, Roig would eventually become one of Barcelona's most prominent cultural figures in the 1980s. Her rich personal history and political awakening are deeply woven into the fabric of her celebrated novel.
